feedbackSignal: Review and user steering showed that goal receipts can leak local user-profile paths and that relying on external PR review to catch them is repeatable workflow failure.
rootOperationalFailure: The goal-board validator checked audit freshness, active route freshness, and review-backfill evidence, but it did not mechanically reject local-only home-directory references in runtime evidence receipts.
durableSystemImprovement: Add a goal-board extension that scans runtime evidence receipts for raw local home paths and fails with redacted diagnostics before future receipts can support route truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Before the R151 cutover receipt exists, the guard scans the latest receipt so the current board cannot keep adding new leaks.
At and after R151, the guard scans every receipt whose id number is at least 151 so a later clean receipt cannot hide an earlier post-cutover leak.
The guard recursively checks string leaves in objects and arrays and reports the receipt id, line number, and JSON path.
The guard rejects Unix, macOS, file URI, percent-decoded file URI, Windows forward-slash, and Windows backslash user-profile paths.
Diagnostics classify the path kind but do not echo the raw local path value.
Historical pre-cutover receipts remain readable once the R151 cutover receipt exists.
Redacted placeholders and repo-relative evidence references pass.
reviewResponse: Broadened matching from one raw prefix to normalized file URI, percent-decoded, Unix/macOS, and Windows user-profile forms.
Changed the post-cutover scan from latest-only to all receipts with id number at least 151.
Specified recursive string-leaf traversal and JSON-path diagnostics.
